summary: Full-stack verification of the new 'Day Trades' feature (backend + frontend) + regression on short-term-growth, top-plays/tracked, and coverage. All acceptance criteria pass. No blocking issues found.

backend: GET /api/day-trades/today → 200. Response shape: {date, session:{phase,note,etTime}, candidates[8], scanned, generatedAt, cached}. session.phase='Closed' (expected, late-night ET).

Each candidate has all required fields: ticker, name, price, changePct, sourceList, technicals{vwap,rsi14,ema9,relVolume,openingRangeHigh}, analyst{consensus,priceTarget,recentActions}, catalyst{headline,sentiment}, score, signals[], risks[], plan{entryZoneLow,entryRef,target1,target2,stop,stopPct,rewardRisk,steps[6]}.

Plan math correct: target1≈entry*1.02 (e.g. CANF 4.63→4.72 = 1.0194), target2≈entry*1.03 (CANF 4.63→4.77 = 1.0302), stopPct within -0.75 to -1.5 (%). Every candidate has rewardRisk>=1 and exactly 6 numbered steps.
First uncached call returned cached=false in ~seconds (result already partially warm), subsequent call cached=true, ?refresh=true returned cached=false (regenerates as spec).

GET /api/day-trades/scoreboard → 200 with {evaluated:0, targetHits:0, stops:0, flat:0, winRatePct:null, avgRealizedPct:null, history:[]}. Empty history is expected (picks only evaluated after their date).
REGRESSION GET /api/universe/short-term-growth?limit=10 → 200. Top-10 growthScore ranked; 7/10 have insiderActivity='Net buying' (ZVRA, DEC, WRD, BCRX, TIGR, QUBT, SBET) — matches upgraded weighting.

REGRESSION GET /api/top-plays/tracked → 200 with active(42), exited(22), bestPerformers(20), stats(closedCount/hitRate/avgReturn/avgWinner/avgLoser/avgHoldDays/reasonBreakdown), exitedForwardSummary, sectorConcentration.
frontend: Login gate (obaidtradez/Odm200429) → dashboard route '/' loads without errors.
Sidebar has 'Day Trades' nav entry under AI INTELLIGENCE (between Top Plays and Future Giants). Clicking routes to /day-trades.
[data-testid=day-trades-page] mounts; [data-testid=session-banner] shows 'Closed · 23:42 ET · Market closed. Review today's trades and prepare tomorrow's plan.'

Exactly 8 [data-testid=day-trade-card-{TICKER}] cards render: CANF, SOC, ICU, SKIL, RAAQ, RNAZ, DSC, GVH. Each shows ticker, change %, source badge ('Top Gainer'), setup score ring (75/etc), catalyst headline + sentiment badge ([data-testid=catalyst-{T}]), 4-cell technicals grid (VWAP / RSI(5M) / OR HIGH / 9-EMA), 4-cell plan grid ([data-testid=trade-plan-{T}]) with Entry Zone / Target 1 (+2%) / Target 2 (+3%) / Stop, plus R/R + analyst consensus/PT line.

First card (CANF) has [data-testid=playbook-CANF] expanded by default; SOC card's playbook is collapsed initially, clicking [data-testid=expand-playbook-SOC] expands and shows 6 numbered steps, 'Why it qualifies' section, and Risk Flags.
[data-testid=day-trades-scoreboard] renders with correct empty-state text 'No evaluated picks yet. Today's picks get scored against real intraday highs/lows after the session closes.'
[data-testid=day-trades-refresh-btn] present (not exercised end-to-end to avoid 60-120s wait; API path already validated).
REGRESSION: /top-plays and / (dashboard) both load successfully, sidebar and content render, no console errors.

test_report_links: existing pytest suite under /app/backend/tests/ was not re-run this iteration (feature covered by direct API validation via curl + JSON schema checks)
critical_code_review_comments: day_trades_engine.py performs ~7 FMP calls per candidate for 16 candidates → 60-120s cold generation. 15-min mongo cache is present, but consider (a) parallelising the per-ticker fetches with an asyncio.gather bounded semaphore, and (b) short-TTL in-process memoisation of /historical-price-eod/full to avoid duplicate hits when tickers overlap between scans. Would reduce refresh latency significantly.

Frontend sidebar 'Day Trades' is rendered as a clickable div/button rather than an anchor tag (`href`). This still works with react-router but breaks middle-click 'open in new tab' and screen-reader link semantics. Consider using <Link to='/day-trades'> or <a href='/day-trades'>.

DayTrades.jsx: the risk disclaimer wording could be more prominent (search for 'disclaim' returned false in body text — 'RISK FLAGS' per card exists, but no top-level 'This is not financial advice' style disclaimer copy was matched). Minor UX/compliance nit, not a bug.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Day-trades scan is expensive (60-120s cold) — always exercise the ?refresh=true path via curl with -m 180+ timeout, or trust the 15-min mongo cache in 'day_trades_cache'. Sidebar 'Day Trades' link is not a real anchor; use page.get_by_text('Day Trades', exact=True).click() or navigate directly to /day-trades. Session is 'Closed' at night ET — that is not a bug. Scoreboard history is intentionally empty until picks age past their session close. First candidate card is expanded by default; use expand-playbook-{2nd ticker} to test toggle behaviour. Top-plays/tracked now has 42 active + 22 exited on this env (up from 44/0 in iteration_8) so exited cards + exitedForwardSummary should now render in the UI — worth verifying if that flow is retested.
rca of the issue: No issues to root-cause. All spec bullets from the review request map 1:1 to observed behaviour.
